notes.note: A famously obscure crux ('de quadrimo Catone'); the words probably point to a passage Cicero had drafted, perhaps for his eulogistic 'Cato' on Cato Uticensis, the reading of which his own hand made hard to decipher.

notes.anchor: only let Publius not have been invited
notes.note: A dinner-party in-joke between Cicero and Tiro: which 'Publius' is meant is now unrecoverable, an unwelcome guest known to both.

notes.anchor: was never really a man of Phaleron at all, but he is now plainly a Billienus
notes.note: A double pun on names. Demetrius of Phalerum was the model statesman-philosopher; 'Billienus' puns on a vulgar Roman name to say this Demetrius is no philosopher but a common rogue.
